title: I Know What You Did Next Xmas

jokes.text: Welcome back the bed-wetter of Building D, Turanga Leela
jokes.setup: Formal introduction of a visiting storyteller
jokes.punchline: The embarrassing childhood detail about bed-wetting
jokes.explanation: Strong character-based humor that establishes both setting and Leela's embarrassing past. Good economy and integration.

jokes.text: We don't got books no more. - What? - He means 'anymore.' Look, sometimes you got to choose between eating and reading, so they ate the books.
jokes.setup: Leela asks what book to read, expects normal answer
jokes.punchline: They literally ate the books due to poverty
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that establishes the orphanage's dire conditions while subverting expectations about reading time.

jokes.text: A... one-eyed princess in a long, flowing... tank top. And she lived in a magical one-room... Castle? Yeah, if you believe the listing agent.
jokes.setup: Traditional fairy tale setup with princess in flowing gown and castle
jokes.punchline: Deflation to mundane reality - tank top, one-room, skeptical real estate reference
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from fairy tale tropes to Leela's actual living situation. Real estate joke is relatable.

jokes.text: And then that's exactly what she did do that. And they all lived happily ever after. The end.
jokes.setup: Expectation of coherent story progression
jokes.punchline: Complete narrative breakdown with redundant, meaningless conclusion
jokes.explanation: Captures Leela's complete inability to storytell through awkward, circular non-ending.

jokes.text: That story was bad. That's not really a question. That wasn't really a story.
jokes.setup: Albert says he has a question about the story
jokes.punchline: Brutal honest critique delivered matter-of-factly, followed by Leela's weak defense and Albert's devastating follow-up
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy in Albert's brutal honesty. Three-beat escalation works perfectly.

jokes.text: Please don't. See you soon.
jokes.setup: Leela promises to return with a better story
jokes.punchline: Albert's blunt 'Please don't' followed by Leela's oblivious 'See you soon'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Albert's directness and Leela's obliviousness.

jokes.text: I just need to think of three of some kind of animal.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to come up with story ideas
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical writing advice about 'three of some kind of animal'
jokes.explanation: Shows Leela's continued confusion about storytelling through absurd pseudo-advice.

jokes.text: Yes. It's the only song I know. Look, friends. I accidentally learned to tap dance.
jokes.setup: Fry justifying playing annoying music
jokes.punchline: It's literally the only song he knows, plus Bender's random tap dance announcement
jokes.explanation: Typical Fry incompetence paired with Bender's random skill acquisition.

jokes.text: Time for my 4:21 daiquiri!
jokes.setup: Professor making his routine announcement
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific time (4:21) for drinking, suggesting rigid alcoholism schedule
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about the Professor's precise scheduling of vices.

jokes.text: Guys, guys, guess who just got commissioned as a tugboat?
jokes.setup: Bender announcing exciting news
jokes.punchline: Being 'commissioned as a tugboat' - treating it like military rank
jokes.explanation: Absurd misuse of military terminology for Bender's job assignment.

jokes.text: Tokyo crazy balls! Yes!
jokes.setup: Bender announcing pachinko break
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic mangling of Japanese pachinko terminology
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from Bender's gambling enthusiasm and cultural ignorance.

jokes.text: I'd like to describe my foot pain. My left has a sharp jabbing throb near the bunion, while the other's got a searing pain like gout. Maybe it's too much albumin because of gravity...
jokes.setup: Professor wants to talk to Leela
jokes.punchline: Launches into gratuitously detailed medical complaints
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor oversharing about medical issues. Good character integration but runs a bit long.

jokes.text: Searing, searing pain that feels as if it were in a vice.
jokes.setup: Leela has left to escape his medical complaints
jokes.punchline: Professor continues describing pain to empty room
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Professor's obliviousness to his audience leaving.

jokes.text: They liked hunger cramps more than your last one.
jokes.setup: Leela confident kids will like her new story more
jokes.punchline: Brutal comparison to physical hunger pain
jokes.explanation: Perfectly cruel and economical way to express how bad Leela's first story was. Dark humor that's very quotable.

jokes.text: Oh, is the organ-harvesting clown here? Nah, Rusty comes on Tuesdays now.
jokes.setup: Casual inquiry about alternative entertainment
jokes.punchline: Treating organ harvesting as routine children's entertainment
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor treating horrific child endangerment as mundane scheduling issue.

jokes.text: He gives me a nice, warm feeling in my eyeballs.
jokes.setup: Child commenting on the brain-scanning bunny
jokes.punchline: Innocent description of what's actually neural manipulation
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from child's innocent description of sinister brain scanning.

jokes.text: Oh, my God. There's Jake Finkelberg! He's so hot. I wish he wasn't my brother.
jokes.setup: Character expressing attraction to someone
jokes.punchline: Casual incest reference in children's programming
jokes.explanation: Shock humor about inappropriate children's TV content, though fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Hurry, Captain! A skinny kid needs our help. Super-size me!
jokes.setup: Superhero scenario about helping children
jokes.punchline: The 'help' is force-feeding junk food to skinny kids
jokes.explanation: Good satire of corporate influence on children's programming and obesity culture.

jokes.text: From the Rowdy Roddy Piper Daycare Center in Glasgow, Scotland, it's Extreme Toddler Wrestling!
jokes.setup: Sports entertainment announcement
jokes.punchline: Combining wrestler name with daycare and toddler violence
jokes.explanation: Absurd juxtaposition of violent entertainment with childcare. Good specificity with Roddy Piper reference.

jokes.text: Don't have any children for the next couple of days.
jokes.setup: Network executive wrapping up brain scanning session
jokes.punchline: Casual warning about reproduction after neural manipulation
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor delivered matter-of-factly about sinister medical side effects.

jokes.text: Can't we just get our organs harvested?
jokes.setup: Child complaining about having to listen to story
jokes.punchline: Preferring organ harvesting to Leela's storytelling
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that shows how bad Leela's stories are by having kids prefer organ harvesting.

jokes.text: Hello. I'm scared of everyones.
jokes.setup: Character introductions in the Humplings segment
jokes.punchline: Timid character with grammar error ('everyones' instead of 'everyone')
jokes.explanation: Mild humor from character trait and grammar mistake typical of children's shows.

jokes.text: I'm a silly spring named Doingg And my bouncing never ends
jokes.setup: Character introducing himself in song
jokes.punchline: Name perfectly matches his sound effect and behavior
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character creation with onomatopoeia integration

jokes.text: What about me? Hey, it's Garbly. Everyone loves him 'cause they can't understand him. It's nice to meet you.
jokes.setup: Introducing character everyone loves
jokes.punchline: They love him specifically because he's incomprehensible, then he speaks clearly
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup where the incomprehensible character speaks perfectly clearly when introduced.

jokes.text: Wow, I can't believe you 'maginated all that stuff.
jokes.setup: Child impressed with Leela's creativity
jokes.punchline: Mispronunciation of 'imagined' as 'maginated'
jokes.explanation: Cute child mispronunciation that works as both character moment and mild wordplay.

jokes.text: their enjoyment glands were squirting like Broadway roadkill!
jokes.setup: TV executive describing positive test results
jokes.punchline: Grotesque anatomical metaphor combined with disturbing imagery
jokes.explanation: Memorably disgusting metaphor that perfectly captures sleazy TV executive character.

jokes.text: Don't I need a degree to write gibberish for toddlers?
jokes.setup: Leela questioning qualifications for TV writing
jokes.punchline: Accurate description of children's TV content as 'gibberish'
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about the low standards of children's television writing.

jokes.text: Come on, Miss Leela. Don't be a fraidy cat like Feffernoose.
jokes.setup: Executive trying to convince Leela to sign
jokes.punchline: References her own fictional character as real example
jokes.explanation: Clever callback showing how the executive absorbed her story elements and treats them as real.

jokes.text: if you just luck out hard enough.
jokes.setup: Leela giving inspirational message to kids about success
jokes.punchline: Attributes success entirely to luck rather than effort
jokes.explanation: Cynical but honest take on success that subverts typical inspirational messaging to children.

jokes.text: Welcome to the glamorous, big-money world of basic cable TV! I'm going to need that pen back.
jokes.setup: Grand announcement about entering glamorous industry
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation with cheap pen retrieval
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation from grandiose promises to petty penny-pinching reality.

jokes.text: These costumes are gonna make it hard to go to the bathroom. I'm not having any problem.
jokes.setup: Fry complaining about costume practicality
jokes.punchline: Bender has no bathroom concerns (being a robot)
jokes.explanation: Simple character-based humor about robot vs human physiology differences.

jokes.text: Sweet zombie Jesus, like it's not hard enough to make a freakin' TV show for under 50 bucks.
jokes.setup: Executive complaining about production challenges
jokes.punchline: Comically low budget of $50 for entire TV show
jokes.explanation: Good characterization of cheap TV production with specific absurdly low number.

jokes.text: Damn it. I'll call you back, Grandma.
jokes.setup: Executive in middle of professional crisis
jokes.punchline: Was apparently on phone with grandmother during work emergency
jokes.explanation: Small character moment showing executive's unprofessional phone habits.

jokes.text: Ach! You writers make me sick. Nice job on the script, though.
jokes.setup: Executive expressing disgust with writers
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction with compliment
jokes.explanation: Typical contradictory behavior from sleazy entertainment executive character.

jokes.text: Cheap lights. Off-brand camera. And non-union action!
jokes.setup: Director calling for action on set
jokes.punchline: Each element emphasizes the low-budget, exploitative production
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating list that parodies film production calls while emphasizing cheap production values.

jokes.text: When I like something, I lick it. Like this raccoon.
jokes.setup: Character explaining his behavior with licking
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates on random raccoon, implying he licked it
jokes.explanation: Absurd children's show logic with implied visual of character holding licked raccoon.

jokes.text: I like turtles!
jokes.setup: Song about not licking living things mentions turtles
jokes.punchline: Reference to internet meme 'I like turtles' kid
jokes.explanation: Simple meme reference that doesn't add much beyond recognition humor.

jokes.text: Poke it with a stick and lick the stick instead.
jokes.setup: Advice about determining if something is alive before licking
jokes.punchline: Suggests bizarre workaround that still involves licking
jokes.explanation: Clever absurd logic that maintains the character's compulsive licking while being 'helpful'

jokes.text: But before we go, let's do everything we just did two more times.
jokes.setup: Ending typical children's show episode
jokes.punchline: Meta reference to how children's shows pad runtime with repetition
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on children's television production shortcuts and time-filling.

jokes.text: Even I have to admire the performance of me, Bender.
jokes.setup: Bender giving compliments about the show
jokes.punchline: Manages to make compliment entirely about himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - even when giving compliments, it's all about his ego.

jokes.text: And it looks so cruddy, their ironic hipster parents will love it.
jokes.setup: Executive explaining the show's appeal
jokes.punchline: Low production values actually attract ironic adult audience
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about ironic consumption of bad media by adult audiences.

jokes.text: Sometimes two or three times.
jokes.setup: Leela noting that good shows get cancelled
jokes.punchline: Reference to shows being cancelled and revived multiple times (like Futurama itself)
jokes.explanation: Meta-reference to Futurama's own cancellation/revival history, earning bonus points for self-awareness.

jokes.text: Baby, I hope you like drowning in caviar, 'cause that is how you're gonna die.
jokes.setup: Doubledeal promising Leela wealth from the show's success
jokes.punchline: Describes luxury food as cause of death
jokes.explanation: Vivid absurdist image of death by luxury - good producer character excess

jokes.text: I interfere with pacemakers.
jokes.setup: Comparison to successful children's character
jokes.punchline: SpongeBob parody character's defining trait is medical device interference
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and dark one-liner that perfectly captures Futurama's robot humor sensibility.

jokes.text: Baby, I hope you like drowning in caviar, 'cause that is how you're gonna die.
jokes.setup: Executive promising wealth and success
jokes.punchline: Hyperbolically specific and morbid metaphor for extreme wealth
jokes.explanation: Memorably bizarre metaphor for success that's both absurd and darkly funny.

jokes.text: But first, the nominees for Best New Kids' Show are... Outstanding Sext Message.
jokes.setup: Awards ceremony for children's programming
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate adult content award mixed with kids' show awards
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate juxtaposition humor typical of adult animation, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: This is Turanga Leela's first nomination and second time in high heels.
jokes.setup: Professional achievement announcement
jokes.punchline: Equally emphasizes trivial personal detail about footwear
jokes.explanation: Deflates prestigious moment with mundane personal detail about Leela's inexperience with formal wear.

jokes.text: I feel so lucky just to have been nominated alongside so many inferior shows.
jokes.setup: Standard humble acceptance speech beginning
jokes.punchline: Backhanded insult disguised as humility
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of typical award speech humility with subtle arrogance.

jokes.text: So, wait. You play a high school student who's also secretly a rock star who's also secretly a massage chair?
jokes.setup: Interviewer trying to understand complex character concept
jokes.punchline: Final reveal that character is furniture
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalating absurdity that parodies overly complex TV character concepts

jokes.text: No. I play a high school student who's also secretly a rock star, but in real life I'm a massage chair. Oh. You ever made out with a tugboat.
jokes.setup: Complex acting role explanation
jokes.punchline: Actor is actually furniture, Bender's inappropriate romantic question
jokes.explanation: Absurdist take on method acting combined with Bender's characteristic crudeness.

jokes.text: Sweet ego of Montego! Someone build a wind farm in front of her mouth!
jokes.setup: Bender complaining about Leela's ego
jokes.punchline: Absurd curse and metaphor for stopping her talking
jokes.explanation: Creative Bender-esque curse and absurd solution to excessive talking. Good character voice.

jokes.text: you're the greatest creative mind since blah, blah, blah-bity blah.
jokes.setup: Executive giving effusive praise
jokes.punchline: Trails off into meaningless sounds, showing insincerity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing the executive's hollow, insincere flattery.

jokes.text: I'll have you know I bedazzle my own underpants.
jokes.setup: Fry defending his creativity after being called 'noncreative'
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and embarrassing craft hobby
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry response - defensive but with hilariously inappropriate example of 'creativity.'

jokes.text: Rumbledy-hump? It's real!
jokes.setup: Leela arrives at mysterious planet location
jokes.punchline: Discovers her made-up fictional world actually exists
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist moment that sets up the central conflict of the episode.

jokes.text: They're like crunchy, green water.
jokes.setup: Character describing vegetables positively
jokes.punchline: Completely unappetizing description that's technically accurate
jokes.explanation: Amusingly accurate but unappetizing description that sounds like children's show dialogue.

jokes.text: I had to be big and strong to fight off the monsters.
jokes.setup: Timid character Feffernoose being described as big and strong
jokes.punchline: Reveals he actually fought monsters, contradicting his fearful nature
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion showing hidden depths to the supposedly timid Feffernoose.

jokes.text: You just wrote down what these space twerps said.
jokes.setup: Bender realizing Leela's creative process
jokes.punchline: Accurately identifies her plagiarism with typical Bender bluntness
jokes.explanation: Bender's characteristic brutal honesty revealing the truth

jokes.text: Leela said a Rumbledy-Hump no-no!
jokes.setup: Leela swears in frustration
jokes.punchline: Childlike creatures treat mild profanity as major transgression
jokes.explanation: Typical children's show reaction to mild adult language, played for cringe humor.

jokes.text: And poo-poo and pee-pee and penis and gay. Those are the 98 words we don't say.
jokes.setup: Song about bad words in children's show context
jokes.punchline: Escalates from bathroom words to inappropriate content, claims there are 98 total
jokes.explanation: Parody of children's show censorship with escalating inappropriateness. Reference to George Carlin's seven words bit.

jokes.text: It's like catching an evangelist in a whorehouse. That was the best Christmas ever.
jokes.setup: Executive comparing Leela's hypocrisy to something
jokes.punchline: Specific inappropriate religious metaphor followed by fond personal memory
jokes.explanation: Good sleazy character moment with dark humor and inappropriate personal revelation.

jokes.text: I'm loving the hypocrisy. It's like catching an evangelist in a whorehouse.
jokes.setup: Bender appreciating Leela's moral contradiction
jokes.punchline: Vivid analogy about religious hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Sharp analogy that perfectly captures Bender's appreciation for moral contradictions

jokes.text: At least Reverend Pickens had the dignity to jump out the whorehouse window with his pants around his ankles.
jokes.setup: Continuing the evangelist/whorehouse comparison
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd detail about the reverend's undignified escape
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with specific absurd visual details that complete the inappropriate story.

jokes.text: He sure has a shiny metal bumbledy-boop.
jokes.setup: Innocent creature describing Bender
jokes.punchline: Childish euphemism for Bender's anatomy
jokes.explanation: Cute children's show style euphemism for describing robot anatomy.

jokes.text: 'Cause now I can get rich off of you. Oh, Lord. From now on, you give me half the gross, or I'll blow your cover.
jokes.setup: Bender expressing love for Leela's scheme
jokes.punchline: Immediately turns it into blackmail opportunity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - turns every situation into personal profit opportunity.

jokes.text: Ugh! You are the worst kind of rich person.
jokes.setup: Leela expressing altruistic motivations despite wealth
jokes.punchline: Bender's disgust at rich person with conscience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line showing his preference for straightforward greed over guilty conscience.

jokes.text: The kids haven't been this excited since Food Day.
jokes.setup: Warden describing kids' excitement level
jokes.punchline: Comparison point is getting food, highlighting orphanage poverty
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about orphanage conditions delivered matter-of-factly.

jokes.text: I do love inconveniencing the underclass.
jokes.setup: Bender standing on poor people
jokes.punchline: Explicitly states his enjoyment of class-based cruelty
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - unapologetically cruel and class-conscious in the most direct way possible.

jokes.text: Is somebody standing on me? Ah. I do love inconveniencing the underclass.
jokes.setup: Bender realizes someone is using him as a step
jokes.punchline: Takes pleasure in being used to oppress others
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender psychology - finds joy in facilitating class oppression

jokes.text: Okay, kids, delta pattern. Go! Go! Go!
jokes.setup: Orphans leaving to dig through trash
jokes.punchline: Treating dumpster diving like military operation
jokes.explanation: Good absurd juxtaposition of military precision applied to scavenging for survival.

jokes.text: Thank you for being my hero.
jokes.setup: Sally expressing gratitude to Leela
jokes.punchline: Sincere emotional moment that makes Leela's fraud more painful
jokes.explanation: Emotional moment that heightens the comedy through dramatic irony - audience knows Leela is a fraud.

jokes.text: Having to wear a costume made from a hollowed-out walrus.
jokes.setup: Leela explaining why she can't continue
jokes.punchline: Reveals disturbing costume construction details
jokes.explanation: Disturbing and absurd detail about low-budget costume construction.

jokes.text: Stealing is a form of creativity. No, it isn't.
jokes.setup: Executive trying to rationalize theft
jokes.punchline: Leela's flat rejection of obvious rationalization
jokes.explanation: Simple back-and-forth showing executive's moral flexibility vs Leela's conscience.

jokes.text: We didn't have a word for it... until now.
jokes.setup: Leela asks if they mean frown
jokes.punchline: Implies her introduction of sadness concept to their world
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist moment about introducing negative emotions to innocent world.

jokes.text: I guess if you can't make up stories, I can't neither.
jokes.setup: Sally losing faith in her abilities
jokes.punchline: Grammar error while expressing lost confidence
jokes.explanation: Sad character moment with grammar error that shows educational impact of Leela's fraud.

jokes.text: No writers, no actors, no problem.
jokes.setup: Executive explaining reality show solution
jokes.punchline: Celebrating elimination of creative professionals
jokes.explanation: Good satire of reality TV's appeal to executives - eliminates expensive creative talent.

jokes.text: I can finally afford my speech therapy.
jokes.setup: Garbly getting paid for the show
jokes.punchline: Plans to fix the trait that made him valuable
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic joke - character wants to eliminate the very trait that makes him commercially valuable.

jokes.text: What orphans? The kids were all adopted.
jokes.setup: Leela worried about teaching orphans bad lesson
jokes.punchline: Reveals the orphans are no longer orphans
jokes.explanation: Good plot twist that undercuts Leela's guilt while creating new moral complications.

jokes.text: You're the boss, Daddy.
jokes.setup: Albert responding to executive's direction
jokes.punchline: Reveals the sleazy executive adopted him
jokes.explanation: Dark humor reveal that the brutally honest critic is now adopted by the exploitative executive.
